steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: The “while” conjunction joins two independent clauses. The first has the finite verb “are taught”; the second reads “students of traditional methods often only ______ such songs at the age of five” and needs a finite verb to function as an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
An independent clause requires a finite verb as its main verb.

Choice A (“begin learning”) provides “begin” as a finite verb in the present tense, with “learning” as its gerund object.

The other three choices offer non-finite verb forms that cannot function as the main verb of an independent clause.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
